digest.component_or_area: store compatibility record + fail-closed capability gate
propositions: Add a committed store-level compatibility record (format_version + required_capabilities) at a fixed path, written atomically, read by a gate that fails closed on unknown version or unrecognised required capability.

The gate is inserted once inside lock.acquire() to cover all lock-based mutations, plus explicit checks in fsck_recover_cli and reconcile._apply_mutations() (after persist=True) for the two paths not routed through the lock.
Four record states are handled: absent = legacy version 0 pass-through, present-compatible = pass, present-incompatible = fail-closed, corrupt/unreadable = fail-closed (never treated as absent).
Read-only and diagnostic commands (fsck diagnostic, show, list, search) remain operable under an incompatible/corrupt record while write/repair paths are blocked before any side effect.
An idempotent ensure unit writes the record when absent on init/fsck-repair/MCP-boot without the covered-write path depending on the fail-silent ensure sweep having run.

Rollback is safe because pre-v1.0 binaries ignore the new .store-compat.json file, proven by a test asserting operability and byte-unchanged record with the gate patched out; CI lint greps lock.acquire(/write_lock( to prevent un-gated write paths.
